reasoning:
Based on the provided Météo-France data, the station at Gourdon recorded a value of 9.4125°C on 2025-10-26, reflecting a short-term cold anomaly against late-October climate normals (typically ~11–13°C mean). 

1. 2025-11-02 (7 days out): With typical early-November means in the 10–11°C range in Gourdon, temperatures are liable to rebound from the 9.4°C cold snap. Reversion to the mean supports a relatively high probability (0.74) for a warmer daily average.

2. 2025-11-25 (~1 month out): Deepening autumn lowers the climatological expected mean significantly (likely around 6.5°C). The probability of exceeding 9.41°C falls short of 0.5 (estimated at 0.42), as temperatures >9.4°C rely on a mild spell.

3. 2026-01-24 (~3 months out): Deep winter entails a lower mean (around 4.5–5°C) but often higher daily variance (standard deviation increasing to ~4-5°C). This larger right-tail variance, compounded by increasing forecast uncertainty over the longer baseline, pushes our epistemic confidence strictly downwards. Per the explicit forecasting rules for horizon horizons, our probability estimation appropriately clusters towards maximum uncertainty (0.47).
